content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • •• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95614

December 7, 1976


Mr. Alan E. Ellis
City Attorney
94 No. Washington St.
Sonora, California 95370

Dear Mr. Ellis:

This is in response to your letter of October 29, 1976, in which you raise four questions regarding Conflict of Interest Codes developed by city agencies for which the City Council is code reviewing body.

With respect to your first two questions, the Political Reform Act does not set any specific mandatory deadline dates for submission of Conflict of Interest Codes. Government Code §87303 reads, in pertinent part:

Each agency shall submit a proposed Conflict of Interest Code to the code reviewing body by the deadline established for the agency by the code reviewing body.

Therefore, it is safe to assume that local code reviewing bodies may exercise their discretion and set reasonable deadlines for agencies for which they are code reviewing body.

In light of the Fair Political Practices Commission's suspension of dates for agencies for which the Commission is code reviewing body in August 1976, and the subsequent extension to February 10, 1977, a reasonable deadline which local code reviewing bodies could have established would have been six months from the date of the Commission's last action, or February 10, 1977.

In response to your third question, designated employees will be required to file disclosure statements within 30 days after the code reviewing body approves the Code for that department.

TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE/ADMINISTRATION (916) 322 5660 • EXECUTIVE/LEGAL 322-5901 • ENFORCEMENT/CONFLICT OF INTEREST 322.6441


Alan E. Ellis
December 8, 1976                                                Page Two


     In answer to your last question, you should complete your
drafting as soon as possible without fear of changes in the
regulations, for that is an area that probably will require
changes for a long time to come, since the Act has only been
in effect since January 1975.  The Act provides procedures
for amending the Codes to comply with any necessary changes
that may occur.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Alfredo Flores
                                        Research Specialist
                                        Conflicts of Interest
                                        Division


AF:gb

X

POLICE DEPARTMENT
(209) 532-7813

CITY CLERK'S OFFICE
(209) 532-4541

FPPC CITY OF SONORA
TUOLUMNE COUNTY
95 No. Washington Street
SONORA, CALIFORNIA
95370

November 2, 1976

October 29, 1976

Fair Political Practices Commission
Conflicts of Interest Division
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Re: Conflict of Interest Codes for
City Departments

Gentlemen:

The City of Sonora is presently in the process of drafting conflict
of interest codes for City Departments and the Planning Commission.
We have had our City Council code approved by FPPC. We are interested
in knowing what the mandatory deadlines are for the codes which the
Council must approve.

Accordingly, would you please advise us by what date we must have
codes for City Departments? Also, by what date must our department
personnel file disclosure forms? The City Council has directed that
we do our best to prepare codes from the latest regulations. Our
past experience has shown that your regulations are subject to
continual change, so we would just as soon begin work with the
latest rules. Therefore, could you please let us know when we
should complete our drafting?

Thank you very much for your assistance.

Very truly yours,

ALAN E. ELLIS
City Attorney for
the City of Sonora

AEE:jt

QUEEN OF THE SOUTHERN MINES
